holdings.infoBox: SPY is an equity ETF with a total of 505 individual holdings. The top holdings are NVIDIA stock at 7.62%, Apple at 6.80%, Microsoft at 4.29%, Amazon.com at 3.63%, and Alphabet at 3.20%.

overview.description: The State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the S&P 500 index. The fund tracks a market cap-weighted index of US large- and mid-cap stocks selected by the S&P Committee. SPY was launched on Jan 22, 1993 and is issued by State Street.

data.title: The S&P 500 is at a critical crossroads. A break lower could signal more losses ahead.
data.text: The S&P 500 on Thursday finished right on the cusp of a critical support line that, if broken, could portend more losses for stocks in the coming days and weeks.

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Shrug Off Elevated Inflation Data as Oil Rebounds
data.text: Both the S&P 500 ETF ($SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF ($QQQ) closed higher on Thursday as investors digested several economic data updates. News That Moved the Stock Market Today…

data.title: These S&P 500 stocks are expected to make double-digit comebacks
data.text: A list of 20 highly rated stocks favored by analysts for gains over the next year includes only one that has risen this year: Nvidia.

data.title: Nasdaq 100, Dow Jones 30 and S&P 500 Forecasts – US Indices Looking to Continue Recovery
data.text: US indices look to continue to go higher on Thursday, continuing the recovery we saw on Wednesday.

data.title: Wall Street is coalescing on a single S&P 500 target. The power of round numbers is irresistible.
data.text: Wall Street, and Main Street, love their round numbers, as at least four strategists have the same 8,000 year-end target on the S&P 500.

data.title: Wall Street Is Getting More Bullish on Stocks. The S&P 500 Is 'Earning' Its Gains.
data.text: Wall Street firms are getting more bullish on earnings—and stocks—heading into the second half of 2026.

data.title: J.P. Morgan lifts its S&P 500 target to 7,800 but warns that a ‘flash crash' remains a risk
data.text: J.P. Morgan strategists lifted their year-end S&P 500 forecast, saying they had been much too cautious around “unpredented” expectations for earnings growth.

data.text: S&P 500 and Dow Jones remain supported by AI strength and easy liquidity, but tariff pressure, sticky inflation, and a hawkish Fed may limit upside in the short term.

data.title: S&P 500 Index: Intel-Apple Chip Deal Sparks Broad Tech Stock Rally
data.text: Intel surged after Apple chose it for U.S. chip production, lifting semiconductor stocks and helping Wall Street rebound from a hawkish Fed selloff.

data.text: US stocks tread water ahead of Kevin Warsh's first Fed decision as investors watch rate policy, the dot plot and support levels in the S&P 500.

data.text: The U.S. stock markets are steady as traders monitor Iran deal developments, key U.S. economic data, and whether tech leadership can continue.

data.title: These stocks have been beating the S&P 500 and could keep the bull market going, says Morgan Stanley
data.text: The bull market for stocks has been dogged by peak earnings revisions breadth and peak liquidity, but fear not says Morgan Stanley.

data.title: Nasdaq 100 and S&P500: Forecast Today as Iran Peace Deal Announcement Lifts US Stocks
data.text: US stocks rally as Iran peace deal announcement sends oil prices lower, while traders await the Fed decision and key economic data.

data.title: S&P 500 Outlook: Using Elliott Wave Analysis to Spot Key Market Turning Points
data.text: A multi-day bounce is underway, nearing its end, to be followed by another decline. Elliott Wave analysis accurately anticipated recent moves, forecasting the three-step corrective bounce.

data.title: S&P 500, SPY, VOO, IVV outlook: top news to watch this week
data.text: The S&P 500 Index and its top ETFs, like State Street's SPY, Vanguard's VOO, and BlackRock's IVV jumped for two consecutive days. It jumped to $7,430, up modestly from this month's low of $7,240.

data.title: S&P 500 and Dow Jones Forecast: SPX Eyes 8,000 as Fed and Tariff Risks Rise
data.text: The S&P 500 and Dow Jones remain bullish, but Fed uncertainty, sticky inflation, tariff risks, and AI spending pressures may make the rally more selective.
